name: Himalayan Spirit
hero_tagline: Yoga at sunrise in Rishikesh, summit at 12,500 ft, Ganga Aarti at dusk.
overview: The Himalayan Spirit is our most popular package for international travelers — a perfect balance of adventure, culture, and spiritual experience compressed into 10 days. You arrive in Delhi and head straight to Rishikesh, where two nights of yoga, rafting, and exploring the Beatles Ashram set the tone. Then it's north into the Himalayan wilderness for the Kedarkantha Trek — one of the most accessible yet rewarding high-altitude treks in India, reaching 12,500 ft with panoramic views across Swargarohini, Bandarpunch, and the Kedarkantha meadows blanketed in snow. The trek ends back in Rishikesh before a final night in Haridwar for the Ganga Aarti ceremony. Private Innova Crysta throughout, certified English-speaking guide, 3-star accommodation in cities, fixed tented camps on trek.
highlights: Summit Kedarkantha at 12,500 ft with 270° Himalayan panorama
White-water rafting on the Ganges in Rishikesh
Beatles Ashram — where the Fab Four composed Abbey Road
Ganga Aarti fire ceremony on the ghats of Haridwar
Private Innova Crysta — no shared transport
ITMB-certified English-speaking mountain guide

itinerary.title: Arrive Delhi — Transfer to Rishikesh
itinerary.desc: Arrive at Indira Gandhi International Airport. Our driver meets you at arrivals with a name board. Private transfer to Rishikesh (approx. 6 hours by road, or overnight drive). Check into your hotel. Welcome briefing with your guide. Evening walk along the Ganges.

itinerary.title: Rishikesh — Yoga, Beatles Ashram, Rafting
itinerary.desc: Morning yoga session at sunrise with a local yoga instructor. Visit the Beatles Ashram (Maharishi Mahesh Yogi Ashram) where the Fab Four composed much of the White Album in 1968. Afternoon white-water rafting on Grade II-III rapids of the Ganges. Evening Ganga Aarti at Triveni Ghat. Stay in Rishikesh.

itinerary.title: Rishikesh — Laxman Jhula, Ram Jhula, Drive to Sankri
itinerary.desc: Morning at leisure — explore Laxman Jhula suspension bridge, local cafes, and the vibrant ashram culture. Post-lunch drive to Sankri (approx. 8 hours) — the base village for Kedarkantha Trek, situated at 6,400 ft in Uttarkashi district. Overnight at guesthouse.

itinerary.title: Acclimatization Day — Sankri Village Walk
itinerary.desc: Rest and acclimatize at Sankri (6,400 ft). Short acclimatization walk to the surrounding forests. Trek briefing with your mountain guide. Equipment check — sleeping bags, trekking poles, gaiters provided. Early dinner and rest. Overnight at Sankri guesthouse.

itinerary.title: Trek Day 1 — Sankri to Juda Ka Talab
itinerary.desc: Trek begins through dense oak and rhododendron forests to Juda Ka Talab — a glacial lake at 9,100 ft that freezes completely in winter. Distance: 4 km, approx. 4 hours. The forest trail is one of the most beautiful parts of the trek. Camp dinner and overnight at Juda Ka Talab.

itinerary.title: Trek Day 2 — Juda Ka Talab to Kedarkantha Base Camp
itinerary.desc: Continue ascending through open meadows to Kedarkantha Base Camp at 11,250 ft. Distance: 4 km, approx. 3-4 hours. Views of Kedarkantha peak dominate the trail. Camp at 11,250 ft — the highest camp of the trek. Clear nights offer spectacular star-gazing.

itinerary.title: Trek Day 3 — Summit Day (12,500 ft) and Descent
itinerary.desc: Pre-dawn start (4 AM) for the summit push. Reach Kedarkantha summit at 12,500 ft by sunrise — panoramic views of Swargarohini, Bandarpunch, Black Peak, and the Kedarkantha meadows. Descend all the way back to Sankri in one long day. Celebration dinner at Sankri guesthouse.

itinerary.title: Drive Sankri to Rishikesh
itinerary.desc: Morning drive back to Rishikesh (approx. 8 hours). Hot shower, good food, and a proper bed await. Evening free for shopping at local markets — pashminas, Himalayan honey, handcrafted items. Overnight in Rishikesh.

itinerary.title: Rishikesh to Haridwar — Ganga Aarti
itinerary.desc: Morning at leisure in Rishikesh. Transfer to Haridwar (approx. 1 hour). Check in, then join the legendary evening Ganga Aarti ceremony at Har Ki Pauri — fire lamps float on the Ganges as priests perform the ritual in front of thousands of pilgrims. One of the most spiritually powerful experiences in India.

itinerary.title: Haridwar to Delhi — Departure
itinerary.desc: Morning at leisure in Haridwar. Visit the ghats at sunrise if time permits. Transfer to Delhi airport by private vehicle (approx. 5 hours). Drop-off at your terminal. Trip ends.

included: All transfers by private Innova Crysta
9 nights accommodation (3-star in Rishikesh & Haridwar, guesthouse in Sankri, fixed tented camps on trek)
Trek equipment: sleeping bag, trekking poles, gaiters
Welcome kit: Junegiri cap, packing guide, SIM card
excluded: Meals in Rishikesh and Haridwar (restaurants nearby at all price points)

faq.q: What fitness level is required for Kedarkantha?
faq.a: Moderate fitness. You should be comfortable walking 4-6 hours per day on uneven terrain. No prior trekking experience is required, but regular walking or gym workouts in the 4-6 weeks before the trek is recommended.

faq.q: What is the best time of year for this package?
faq.a: October-November for clear skies and autumn colours. January-March for deep snow and white landscapes. April-May for spring flowers. June-September is monsoon — trek is closed.

faq.q: Is the trek safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes. Our guides are trained, the trail is well-marked, and we have a safety protocol for all trekkers. Many solo female international travelers have done this trek with us without any issues.

faq.q: What is the highest altitude on this trek?
faq.a: Kedarkantha summit is at 12,500 ft (3,810 m). Base camp is at 11,250 ft. Altitude sickness is uncommon but we carry a full medical kit and acclimatize properly on Day 4.

faq.q: Can I extend this package?
faq.a: Yes — popular extensions include Har Ki Dun Trek (+5 days), Varanasi (+3 days), or Taj Mahal day trip from Delhi. WhatsApp us for a custom itinerary.

testimonials.quote: The Kedarkantha summit was the single most breathtaking moment of my life. Junegiri's organisation was flawless — private car, great guide, no hidden costs. I'll be booking the 15-day trek next year.
testimonials.author: James T.

testimonials.quote: I was nervous about solo trekking in India. Our guide Arun was knowledgeable, funny, and kept us safe throughout. The Ganga Aarti on the last evening was magical.
testimonials.author: Nina K.

testimonials.quote: Did this with my partner for our anniversary. Perfectly balanced — yoga and culture first, then the wild Himalayas. Worth every dollar.
testimonials.author: Mark & Sarah D.

guide_credentials: ITMB Certified Mountain Guide, 12 years experience, Kedarkantha specialist

name: Yoga & Chopta Trek
hero_tagline: Four days of deep Rishikesh yoga, then the world's highest Shiva temple at 13,000 ft.
overview: The Yoga & Chopta Trek is built for travelers who want both inner and outer transformation. You spend four immersive days in Rishikesh — the yoga capital of the world — with morning yoga and meditation, Ayurveda consultations, cooking classes, rafting on the Ganges, and evenings exploring ashrams. Then you drive into the Garhwal Himalayas to Chopta — Uttarakhand's 'mini Switzerland' — for the Tungnath Trek, which leads to the world's highest Shiva temple at 12,073 ft and the Chandrashila summit at 13,123 ft with 270-degree Himalayan views. This package is particularly popular with travelers from Europe and the USA who combine wellness with adventure.
highlights: 4 days intensive yoga, meditation, and Ayurveda in Rishikesh
Chandrashila summit at 13,123 ft — highest point of the trek
Tungnath Temple — world's highest Shiva temple
White-water rafting on Grade II-III Ganges rapids
Chopta meadows — rhododendron and pine at 8,000 ft
Ganga Aarti at Haridwar — India's most sacred river ritual

itinerary.title: Arrive Delhi — Transfer to Rishikesh
itinerary.desc: Arrive at Delhi airport, private transfer to Rishikesh (6 hours). Check in, orientation walk along the Ganges. Rest.

itinerary.title: Rishikesh Yoga Day 1 — Foundations
itinerary.desc: Morning yoga and pranayama (breathing) at sunrise with certified instructor. Afternoon: Beatles Ashram visit, Ayurveda introduction session. Evening Ganga Aarti at Triveni Ghat.

itinerary.title: Rishikesh Yoga Day 2 — Deepening
itinerary.desc: Morning yoga and meditation session. Afternoon: White-water rafting on the Ganges (Grade II-III rapids, 16 km). Evening: Ashram tour — Ram Jhula, Parmarth Niketan, local spiritual culture.

itinerary.title: Rishikesh Yoga Day 3 — Integration
itinerary.desc: Morning yoga and nidra (sleep meditation). Afternoon cooking class — learn to make a full Himalayan dal-sabzi meal. Evening: Laxman Jhula walk, café time, journaling.

itinerary.title: Drive Rishikesh to Chopta (Ukhimath route)
itinerary.desc: Scenic 5-6 hour drive through Devprayag, Rudraprayag, and Ukhimath to Chopta at 8,000 ft. The road passes three sacred river confluences. Check into camp. Trek briefing and early rest.

itinerary.title: Acclimatization — Chopta Meadows Walk
itinerary.desc: Rest day at Chopta. Short 3 km meadow walk through rhododendron and buransh forest at 8,000-9,000 ft. Views of Kedarnath, Chaukhamba, and the Garhwal range on clear days. Equipment check and briefing.

itinerary.title: Trek — Tungnath Temple (12,073 ft) + Chandrashila Summit (13,123 ft)
itinerary.desc: Pre-dawn start at 4 AM from Chopta. Trek 3.5 km to Tungnath — world's highest Shiva temple, believed to be 1,000+ years old. Continue 1 km to Chandrashila summit at 13,123 ft. 270-degree views: Kedarnath, Chaukhamba, Nanda Devi, Trishul, Bandarpunch all visible on clear days. Descend back to Chopta. Celebration dinner at camp.

itinerary.title: Drive Chopta to Haridwar
itinerary.desc: Morning drive through the scenic Kedarnath Wildlife Sanctuary back to Haridwar (approx. 5-6 hours). Check in, rest, explore the ghats.

itinerary.title: Haridwar — Ganga Aarti, Har Ki Pauri
itinerary.desc: Morning visit to Mansa Devi Temple (cable car). Evening Ganga Aarti at Har Ki Pauri — the most sacred of all Ganga Aartis, attended by thousands. Farewell dinner.

itinerary.title: Haridwar to Delhi — Departure
itinerary.desc: Private transfer to Delhi airport (5 hours). Drop at your terminal. Trip ends.

included: All transfers by private Innova Crysta
9 nights accommodation (3-star in Rishikesh & Haridwar, tented camp at Chopta)
4 yoga and meditation sessions with certified instructor
ITMB-certified English guide for trek days
Trek equipment: trekking poles, gaiters, sleeping bag
excluded: Meals in Rishikesh and Haridwar (restaurants nearby)

faq.q: Do I need prior yoga experience?
faq.a: No. Our yoga sessions are adapted for all levels from complete beginners to experienced practitioners. The instructor will personalise sessions based on your level.

faq.q: What is Chandrashila's altitude and is it hard?
faq.a: Chandrashila is at 13,123 ft (4,000 m). The trek from Chopta is 4.5 km one way with 1,500 ft elevation gain. Moderate difficulty — suitable for fit beginners with proper acclimatization.

faq.q: Is rafting mandatory?
faq.a: No — rafting is included in the itinerary but optional. Let us know in advance if you prefer to skip it and we can arrange an alternative (Ayurveda massage, additional yoga session, etc.).

faq.q: What's the best season for this package?
faq.a: March-April (spring flowers, clear views), September-November (post-monsoon clarity, best for summit views). December-February is snow season — beautiful but cold.

testimonials.quote: This was the perfect India trip. Four days of yoga changed my relationship with stress, then the Chandrashila summit was the physical equivalent. Junegiri planned every detail perfectly.
testimonials.author: Claire B.

testimonials.quote: I'm a yoga teacher and this package exceeded my expectations. The Rishikesh instructors were world-class. The Chopta meadows at dawn are incomparable.
testimonials.author: Maria V.

testimonials.quote: The cooking class was an unexpected highlight — I'm now making dal back home every week. The summit views were worth every step.
testimonials.author: Tom R.

guide_credentials: ITMB Certified, Yoga retreat specialist, 9 years leading international groups in Garhwal

name: Himalayan Village Trail
destinations_short: Delhi · Kasol · Kheerganga · Triund · McLeod Ganj · Amritsar
hero_tagline: Parvati Valley hot springs, Triund at midnight, Dalai Lama's temple, Golden Temple at sunrise.
overview: The Himalayan Village Trail follows one of the most beloved backpacker routes in Asia — yet with Junegiri's private transport and curated accommodation, you experience it without the chaos. From Delhi you head north to Kasol in the Parvati Valley — the most iconic destination for Israeli, European, and Australian travelers in Himachal Pradesh. Trek to Kheerganga's natural hot spring at 9,711 ft, then cross to McLeod Ganj (Dharamsala) — home of the Dalai Lama and the Tibetan Government in Exile. Trek up to Triund for one of the finest overnight camp experiences in the Himalayas. End the journey at Amritsar's Golden Temple — the spiritual heart of Sikhism — before returning to Delhi. This route is especially popular with Israeli travelers and young European and Australian adventurers.
highlights: Kheerganga hot spring at 9,711 ft — natural hot spring in the Himalayas
Triund overnight camp — panoramic Dhauladhar range at sunrise
Dalai Lama Complex, McLeod Ganj — Tibetan culture and Buddhism
Golden Temple, Amritsar — most visited site in India
Wagah Border ceremony — India-Pakistan flag-lowering at sunset
Kasol village — the Himachal backpacker capital

itinerary.title: Arrive Delhi
itinerary.desc: Arrive at IGI Airport. Private transfer to hotel in Delhi. Rest and recover from journey. Welcome dinner at a local restaurant.

itinerary.title: Delhi to Kasol (via Chandigarh)
itinerary.desc: Early morning private transfer to Kasol via Chandigarh and Bhuntar (approx. 9-10 hours). Stop in Bhuntar for lunch. Arrive in Kasol by evening — the Parvati River views from the guesthouse are spectacular. Local Israeli restaurants and Israeli bakeries nearby.

itinerary.title: Kasol — Parvati Valley Exploration
itinerary.desc: Free morning in Kasol — cafes, local market, Parvati River walk. Afternoon short hike to Chalal village (30 min walk) — a quieter, more local experience. Evening back in Kasol. Plenty of options for vegetarian food, hummus, and falafel.

itinerary.title: Trek — Kasol to Kheerganga (9,711 ft)
itinerary.desc: Trek from Nakthan to Kheerganga — 12 km, approx. 5-6 hours through dense forests and Himalayan villages. Arrive at Kheerganga's famous natural hot spring pool at 9,711 ft. Soak in the hot spring as the sun sets over the mountains. Overnight at camp.

itinerary.title: Kheerganga — Morning Soak + Descent to Kasol
itinerary.desc: Morning dip in the hot springs at sunrise — one of the most peaceful moments of the trip. Descend back through the forest to Nakthan, then transfer to Bhuntar. Drive to McLeod Ganj (approx. 4 hours). Check in.

itinerary.title: McLeod Ganj — Dalai Lama Complex + Tibetan Culture
itinerary.desc: Morning visit to the Dalai Lama Complex — Tsuglagkhang Temple, Tibet Museum, and the monastery where you may attend a public teaching if scheduled. Afternoon: Tibetan market, momos lunch, Namgyal Monastery. Evening: local cafe culture in McLeod Ganj.

itinerary.title: Trek — McLeod Ganj to Triund (9,350 ft)
itinerary.desc: Trek from McLeod Ganj to Triund — 9 km, approx. 4 hours. The Dhauladhar range towers above you throughout the climb. Arrive at Triund meadow at 9,350 ft. Sunset over the plains of Punjab on one side, Dhauladhar peaks on the other. Overnight at fixed camp.

itinerary.title: Triund Sunrise + Drive to Amritsar
itinerary.desc: Wake at 5 AM for the Triund sunrise — one of the finest in Himachal Pradesh. Descent after breakfast, then drive to Amritsar via Pathankot (approx. 4-5 hours). Check in at hotel in Amritsar.

itinerary.title: Amritsar — Golden Temple at Dawn + Wagah Border
itinerary.desc: 5 AM visit to Harmandir Sahib (Golden Temple) before crowds arrive — the reflection of gold on the sacred pool in early morning light is unforgettable. Free time for the Amritsar langar (world's largest free community kitchen — 100,000 meals/day). Evening: Wagah Border ceremony — India-Pakistan flag lowering with marching soldiers and enormous crowd energy.

itinerary.title: Amritsar — Jallianwala Bagh + Partition Museum
itinerary.desc: Morning visit to Jallianwala Bagh (historic site of 1919 massacre). The Partition Museum — the world's first museum dedicated to the Partition of India — is essential for understanding modern South Asia. Afternoon at leisure. Overnight in Amritsar.

itinerary.title: Amritsar to Delhi (Train or Flight)
itinerary.desc: Transfer to Amritsar station or airport. Train to Delhi (5.5 hours) or flight (1.5 hours) depending on your preference. Afternoon/evening in Delhi — Connaught Place, India Gate, or rest before final departure.

itinerary.title: Delhi — Departure
itinerary.desc: Private transfer to IGI Airport. Trip ends. Optional: add a Taj Mahal day trip from Delhi if your flight departs late.

included: All transfers by private Innova Crysta
11 nights accommodation (hotels in Delhi, Kasol, McLeod Ganj, Amritsar; fixed camps at Kheerganga and Triund)
All meals on trek days (Days 4-5, Day 7)
Trek equipment: sleeping bag, trekking poles
Wagah Border ceremony (guided, with seating arrangement)
excluded: Meals in Kasol, McLeod Ganj, Amritsar (abundant restaurant options at all budgets)
Amritsar-Delhi train/flight ticket (approx. $20-60 depending on mode)

faq.q: Is this route popular with Israeli travelers?
faq.a: Yes — this is the most iconic route for Israeli post-army travelers. Kasol has been a hub for Israeli backpackers for 20+ years. Hebrew menus, Israeli bakeries, and a strong community atmosphere.

faq.q: What is the trek difficulty?
faq.a: Easy-Moderate. Kheerganga is a straightforward forest trail (12 km, 5 hours) and Triund is a popular day trek (9 km, 4 hours). No technical sections. Suitable for fit beginners.

faq.q: Are vegetarian options available throughout?
faq.a: Yes — Kasol and McLeod Ganj are among the best vegetarian dining destinations in India. Tibetan cuisine in Dharamsala and Israeli-influenced cafes in Kasol make it easy.

faq.q: Can I extend to Spiti Valley from McLeod Ganj?
faq.a: Yes — Spiti Valley is accessible from McLeod Ganj in 1-2 days. We offer a 3-5 day Spiti extension. WhatsApp us to discuss.

testimonials.quote: This is the perfect India route. Kasol, Kheerganga, Triund, Amritsar — everything an Israeli wants. Junegiri added the organisation we needed. Highly recommend for groups.
testimonials.author: Yael R.

testimonials.quote: The Golden Temple at 5 AM is one of the most beautiful things I have ever witnessed. The whole trip was extraordinary. Private car made everything so much more comfortable.
testimonials.author: Anna S.

testimonials.quote: I came for Kasol, stayed for everything else. The Wagah Border was electric. Junegiri's guide knew every corner of every place we visited.
testimonials.author: Ben T.

guide_credentials: ITMB Certified, Himachal Pradesh specialist, 11 years leading international groups

name: Sacred India Circuit
hero_tagline: Taj Mahal at sunrise, Ganga Aarti at dusk, and Kedarnath's ancient temple at 11,755 ft.
overview: The Sacred India Circuit combines India's two most iconic experiences — the Taj Mahal and the Himalayan pilgrimage to Kedarnath — into one seamless 12-day journey. You arrive in Delhi and head immediately to Agra for the Taj Mahal at sunrise, before driving to Rishikesh for two nights of yoga, rafting, and the Ganges. Then into the high Himalayas for the Kedarnath Yatra — a 16 km trek to the most sacred Shiva shrine in India, set against a cirque of snow-capped peaks at 11,755 ft. Return through Haridwar for the Ganga Aarti before flying home from Delhi. This is the definitive bucket-list India experience, and the most popular package for UK, USA, and Australian travelers visiting India for the first time.
highlights: Taj Mahal at sunrise — world's most iconic monument
Kedarnath Temple at 11,755 ft — one of the 12 Jyotirlingas
White-water rafting on the Ganges in Rishikesh
Ganga Aarti at Har Ki Pauri, Haridwar
Agra Fort and Mehtab Bagh (Taj Mahal from the other side of the river)
Kedarnath shrine — the spiritual heart of the Himalayas

itinerary.title: Arrive Delhi — Old Delhi Immersion
itinerary.desc: Arrive at IGI Airport. Private transfer to central Delhi. Afternoon orientation walk: Jama Masjid, Old Delhi lanes, Chandni Chowk street food. Welcome dinner. Rest.

itinerary.title: Agra — Taj Mahal Sunrise + Agra Fort
itinerary.desc: Pre-dawn drive to Agra (2.5 hours). Arrive at the Taj Mahal gates as they open at sunrise — the most beautiful light of the day. Private guide explains the love story of Shah Jahan and Mumtaz. Afternoon: Agra Fort (Red Fort), Mehtab Bagh sunset view of the Taj. Evening drive to Rishikesh (6 hours).

itinerary.title: Rishikesh — Yoga, Beatles Ashram
itinerary.desc: Arrive in Rishikesh by morning. Morning yoga session with local instructor. Visit the Beatles Ashram — walk the meditation cells where Lennon, McCartney, Harrison, and Starr wrote 48 songs. Evening Ganga Aarti at Triveni Ghat.

itinerary.title: Rishikesh — Rafting, Laxman Jhula
itinerary.desc: Morning white-water rafting on Grade II-III Ganges rapids (16 km). Afternoon: explore Laxman Jhula, Ram Jhula, ashram hopping. Late afternoon drive to Sonprayag (5-6 hours) — the staging point for Kedarnath.

itinerary.title: Rest Day at Sonprayag / Gaurikund (6,562 ft)
itinerary.desc: Acclimatize at Gaurikund. Visit the ancient Gauri Temple and hot spring. Trek briefing. Early dinner and rest before the big trek day.

itinerary.title: Kedarnath Trek — Gaurikund to Kedarnath (11,755 ft)
itinerary.desc: The most important day. Trek from Gaurikund (6,562 ft) to Kedarnath Temple (11,755 ft) — 16 km, 5,000 ft elevation gain, approx. 6-7 hours. Pony and helicopter options available (at extra cost). Arrive at Kedarnath — the ancient stone temple surrounded by Himalayan peaks. Evening puja (prayer ceremony). Overnight at lodge near the temple.

itinerary.title: Kedarnath — Dawn Puja + Descent
itinerary.desc: 5 AM — attend the pre-dawn abhishek (sacred ritual bathing of the Shivalinga) inside the Kedarnath Temple — one of the most sacred rituals in Hinduism. Trek descent (16 km down, approx. 5 hours). Drive to Guptkashi or Rudraprayag for overnight.

itinerary.title: Drive to Haridwar
itinerary.desc: Scenic drive back through the Alaknanda and Ganga valleys to Haridwar (approx. 5-6 hours). Stop at Devprayag — the confluence of Alaknanda and Bhagirathi rivers where the Ganga officially begins. Check into hotel in Haridwar.

itinerary.title: Haridwar — Ganga Aarti + Ghats
itinerary.desc: Morning visit to Har Ki Pauri ghats — witness pilgrims bathing at sunrise. Mansa Devi Temple (cable car). Afternoon at leisure. Evening: the legendary Ganga Aarti at Har Ki Pauri — bells, fire, priests, and 10,000 devotees on the river steps.

itinerary.title: Haridwar to Delhi
itinerary.desc: Private transfer to Delhi (5 hours). Afternoon at leisure — Humayun's Tomb, India Gate, Lodhi Gardens, or shopping at Janpath/Dilli Haat.

itinerary.title: Delhi — Cultural Day
itinerary.desc: Morning: Qutub Minar (12th century UNESCO monument), Mehrauli Archaeological Park. Afternoon: Raj Ghat (Gandhi memorial), National Museum (optional). Farewell dinner — North Indian cuisine.

itinerary.title: Departure from Delhi
itinerary.desc: Private transfer to IGI Airport. Trip ends.

included: All transfers by private Innova Crysta
11 nights accommodation (3-star hotels in Delhi, Agra, Rishikesh, Haridwar; guesthouse/lodge near Kedarnath)
Taj Mahal private guide and entry tickets
excluded: Meals in Delhi, Agra, Rishikesh, Haridwar (restaurants nearby)
Kedarnath helicopter (optional, ₹8,000-10,000 if preferred over trek)
Pony for Kedarnath trek (optional, ₹2,500 one way)

pricing.label: Solo Traveler
pricing.note: Single room, private vehicle, solo guide

faq.q: Is the Kedarnath trek hard?
faq.a: It's 16 km uphill with 5,000 ft elevation gain — the hardest physical day of this package. However, ponies and helicopters are available as alternatives. Good fitness and acclimatization on Day 5 make it manageable for most travelers.

faq.q: When is Kedarnath Temple open?
faq.a: Kedarnath Temple opens in late April/early May and closes in November (exact dates follow the Hindu calendar). Best visiting period: May-June and September-October. July-August is monsoon — trek is open but wet.

faq.q: Can I see the Taj Mahal on the same trip as Kedarnath?
faq.a: Yes — this package is specifically designed to combine both. You see the Taj Mahal on Day 2 en route to Rishikesh, then do the Kedarnath pilgrimage on Days 6-7.

faq.q: Is this a religious trip or can non-Hindus visit Kedarnath?
faq.a: Non-Hindus are warmly welcomed at Kedarnath. The temple is open to all. Many international travelers describe it as a deeply moving spiritual experience regardless of their own faith.

testimonials.quote: From the Taj Mahal at dawn to Kedarnath at 11,000 feet — this trip showed me more of India in 12 days than I could have imagined. The guide was extraordinary.
testimonials.author: Rachel K.

testimonials.quote: The dawn puja at Kedarnath was one of the most moving experiences of my life. I'm not religious, but standing in a 7th-century stone temple as the priests chant at 5 AM — it gets you.
testimonials.author: David M.

testimonials.quote: Perfectly organised. The Taj Mahal guide was superb — told us things no guidebook mentions. Everything flowed seamlessly.
testimonials.author: Emma & Paul T.

guide_credentials: ITMB Certified, Heritage guide (Delhi/Agra), Kedarnath yatra specialist, 15 years

name: Valley of Flowers Grand Tour
destinations_short: Delhi · Agra · Rishikesh · Valley of Flowers · Haridwar · Jaipur
hero_tagline: The Taj Mahal, a valley of 500 Himalayan wildflower species, Ganga Aarti, and Rajasthan's Pink City.
overview: The Valley of Flowers Grand Tour is our premium 14-day flagship for travelers who want to see India's absolute best in one journey — from the marble perfection of the Taj Mahal to the unreal floral landscape of the Valley of Flowers UNESCO World Heritage Site, via Rishikesh's yoga and rafting, the Hemkund Sahib sacred lake, and Jaipur's forts and bazaars. The Valley of Flowers blooms between July and September with over 500 species of wildflowers at 11,000-14,000 ft — an experience found nowhere else on earth. This package is designed for travelers who want cultural depth, natural wonder, and physical adventure in one seamless itinerary. Most popular with UK, USA, and Australian travelers on their first serious India trip.
highlights: Valley of Flowers — 500+ species of Himalayan wildflowers, UNESCO World Heritage
Hemkund Sahib — sacred Sikh lake at 15,190 ft (highest altitude in the trip)
Taj Mahal sunrise — world's most iconic monument
Rishikesh yoga and Ganges rafting
Jaipur — Amber Fort, City Palace, bazaars of Rajasthan
Haridwar Ganga Aarti — India's most sacred river ritual

itinerary.title: Arrive Delhi
itinerary.desc: Private airport pickup, transfer to hotel, welcome briefing.

itinerary.title: Agra — Taj Mahal Sunrise
itinerary.desc: Drive to Agra at dawn (2.5 hours). Taj Mahal as gates open. Private guide, Agra Fort, Mehtab Bagh. Evening drive to Rishikesh.

itinerary.title: Rishikesh — Yoga + Beatles Ashram
itinerary.desc: Yoga at sunrise. Beatles Ashram. Evening Ganga Aarti at Triveni Ghat.

itinerary.title: Rishikesh — Rafting + Drive to Joshimath
itinerary.desc: Morning rafting on Ganges. Post-lunch drive to Joshimath (8-9 hours, 6,150 ft). Arrival, check in, rest.

itinerary.title: Joshimath — Acclimatization + Govindghat
itinerary.desc: Rest at Joshimath. Short walk to Narasimha Temple. Afternoon drive to Govindghat — the trailhead for Valley of Flowers. Trek briefing. Early rest.

itinerary.title: Trek Day 1 — Govindghat to Ghangaria (10,800 ft)
itinerary.desc: Trek from Govindghat to Ghangaria — 13 km through Bhyundar Valley, following the Pushpawati river. Arrive at Ghangaria, the base camp for both Valley of Flowers and Hemkund Sahib. Overnight at guesthouse.

itinerary.title: Valley of Flowers — Day Visit (12,300 ft)
itinerary.desc: Day hike into the Valley of Flowers UNESCO World Heritage Site — 3 km from Ghangaria. The valley stretches 8 km and blooms with Brahmakamal, Himalayan blue poppy, orchids, and hundreds of wildflower species between July-September. Return to Ghangaria.

itinerary.title: Hemkund Sahib — Sacred Lake (15,190 ft)
itinerary.desc: Trek to Hemkund Sahib — a sacred Sikh lake at 15,190 ft surrounded by seven snow peaks. The Gurdwara Sahib here is one of the holiest Sikh shrines. The altitude is demanding (2,400 ft gain from Ghangaria) — rewarded by an extraordinary experience. Return to Ghangaria.

itinerary.title: Trek Back — Ghangaria to Govindghat + Drive to Haridwar
itinerary.desc: Trek down to Govindghat (13 km). Drive to Haridwar (approx. 7 hours). Check into hotel, rest.

itinerary.title: Haridwar — Ganga Aarti + Ghats
itinerary.desc: Morning at ghats, Mansa Devi Temple. Evening Ganga Aarti at Har Ki Pauri.

itinerary.title: Haridwar to Jaipur (via Delhi or direct)
itinerary.desc: Transfer to Delhi, then evening train to Jaipur (Pink City Express — 4.5 hours, scenic). Arrive Jaipur, check in.

itinerary.title: Jaipur — Amber Fort + City Palace
itinerary.desc: Morning: Amber Fort (elephant ride optional), Jal Mahal. Afternoon: City Palace complex, Jantar Mantar (UNESCO astronomical observatory). Evening: Johari Bazaar for gemstones and textiles.

itinerary.title: Jaipur — Hawa Mahal + Local Life
itinerary.desc: Hawa Mahal (Palace of Winds). Visit a blue pottery workshop. Local market for block-print textiles, Rajasthani crafts. Farewell Rajasthani thali dinner.

itinerary.title: Jaipur to Delhi — Departure
itinerary.desc: Train or drive to Delhi (4.5 hours). Transfer to IGI Airport. Trip ends.

included: All transfers by private Innova Crysta
13 nights accommodation (3-star hotels in Delhi, Agra, Rishikesh, Joshimath, Haridwar, Jaipur; guesthouse at Ghangaria)
Taj Mahal private guide and entry
ITMB-certified English guide throughout trek section
excluded: Haridwar-Jaipur train ticket (~₹800-2,000 depending on class)

pricing.label: Solo Traveler
pricing.note: Single room, private vehicle, solo guide

faq.q: When does the Valley of Flowers bloom?
faq.a: Peak bloom: mid-July to mid-August. The valley is accessible June through October, but the floral display is most spectacular in July-August. September has fewer flowers but clearer skies.

faq.q: Is Hemkund Sahib at 15,190 ft safe?
faq.a: Yes with proper acclimatization. Day 5 at Joshimath (6,150 ft) and Day 6 at Ghangaria (10,800 ft) are essential acclimatization steps. Altitude sickness medication (Diamox) is available and we carry a full medical kit.

faq.q: Can I skip Jaipur and add more trek time?
faq.a: Yes — we can swap Jaipur for an extra day at the Valley of Flowers, a side trek to Kuari Pass, or Varanasi. WhatsApp us for a custom version.

testimonials.quote: I've been to 47 countries. The Valley of Flowers is the single most beautiful place I have ever seen. And Junegiri made the entire 14-day journey feel effortless.
testimonials.author: Jonathan F.

testimonials.quote: Taj Mahal to Hemkund Sahib in two weeks — India in its full glory. The guide who took us through the valley knew every flower by name. Extraordinary experience.
testimonials.author: Charlotte W.

testimonials.quote: The Jaipur addition was perfect. We went from Himalayan altitude to Rajput forts in 48 hours. India is overwhelming in the best possible way.
testimonials.author: Lars E.

guide_credentials: ITMB Certified, Valley of Flowers specialist since 2008, 16 years guiding international groups

name: Ultimate Himalayan Expedition
destinations_short: Delhi · Rishikesh · Har Ki Dun · Mussoorie · Kasol · Amritsar
hero_tagline: 15 days. Two states. Three Himalayan regions. The complete Indian adventure.
overview: The Ultimate Himalayan Expedition is for travelers who want to go deep — not just check boxes. In 15 days you cover Uttarakhand's most remote forest trek (Har Ki Dun — the Valley of Gods), the colonial hill station of Mussoorie, the legendary Parvati Valley backpacker scene in Kasol/Himachal Pradesh, and the spiritual and historical weight of Amritsar's Golden Temple. This is India's ultimate adventure circuit: Garhwal wilderness, Himalayan culture, Sikh spirituality, and the Punjab plains — all in a seamless private journey. The Har Ki Dun Trek takes you through dense oak and pine forests to a high-altitude valley that locals call the abode of the gods, with the Swargarohini and Bandarpunch glaciers as your backdrop.
highlights: Har Ki Dun Trek — Valley of Gods, 11,680 ft, completely remote wilderness
Swargarohini peak views — the mythological site of the Pandavas' ascent to heaven
Rishikesh yoga, Beatles Ashram, Ganges rafting
Mussoorie — colonial hill station, Mall Road, Kempty Falls
Kasol and Parvati Valley — Himalayan backpacker haven
Golden Temple Amritsar — most visited monument in India

itinerary.title: Arrive Delhi
itinerary.desc: Private airport pickup. Transfer to hotel. Welcome briefing.

itinerary.title: Rishikesh — Yoga + Beatles Ashram
itinerary.desc: Drive to Rishikesh (6 hours). Yoga at sunset. Beatles Ashram. Evening Ganga Aarti.

itinerary.title: Rishikesh — Rafting + Drive to Sankri
itinerary.desc: Morning rafting. Post-lunch drive to Sankri (8 hours, 6,400 ft) — Har Ki Dun base.

itinerary.title: Acclimatization at Sankri
itinerary.desc: Rest and short walk to Osla village. Trek briefing. Early rest.

itinerary.title: Trek Day 1 — Sankri to Taluka (7,480 ft)
itinerary.desc: Trek from Sankri to Taluka — 7 km through apple orchards and terraced fields. Taluka is the last motorable point on the Har Ki Dun trail.

itinerary.title: Trek Day 2 — Taluka to Seema (8,800 ft)
itinerary.desc: Trek along the Supin river through dense cedar, birch, and oak forest. Pass Gangad village — unchanged for centuries. 12 km, approx. 6 hours.

itinerary.title: Trek Day 3 — Seema to Har Ki Dun (11,680 ft)
itinerary.desc: Final ascent to Har Ki Dun Valley — a hanging valley directly beneath Swargarohini. The Pandavas, according to Hindu mythology, ascended to heaven from this valley. Views of Swargarohini (20,722 ft) and Bandarpunch (20,154 ft). Camp overnight in the valley.

itinerary.title: Har Ki Dun — Rest and Exploration Day
itinerary.desc: Day hike to Maninda Tal (optional, 4 km) or simply explore the valley — meadows, glacial streams, alpine birds. One of the most remote and peaceful places accessible without technical climbing.

itinerary.title: Return Trek — Har Ki Dun to Sankri
itinerary.desc: Full return trek, often done in one day (descending is faster). 26 km over the two-day route, approx. 8 hours. Celebration dinner at Sankri.

itinerary.title: Drive Sankri to Mussoorie
itinerary.desc: Drive from Sankri to Mussoorie (approx. 6-7 hours). Mussoorie sits at 6,588 ft — the Queen of Hill Stations, first established by the British in 1823. Check in, Mall Road evening walk.

itinerary.title: Mussoorie — Kempty Falls + Lal Tibba
itinerary.desc: Morning: Kempty Falls (15 km from Mussoorie). Afternoon: Lal Tibba — the highest point in Mussoorie with telescope views of the Himalayan range. Cloud's End walk. Evening: Mall Road shopping — woolens, local honey.

itinerary.title: Drive Mussoorie to Kasol
itinerary.desc: Long scenic drive via Chandigarh to Kasol in Parvati Valley (approx. 8-9 hours). Check in to guesthouse. Evening walk along the Parvati river.

itinerary.title: Kasol — Parvati Valley + Kheerganga Hike
itinerary.desc: Morning exploration of Kasol, Chalal village, and the river. Afternoon short hike (4 km) toward Kheerganga forest trail or Nakthan. Israeli cafes, fusion food, local market. Overnight in Kasol.

itinerary.title: Kasol to Amritsar — Golden Temple
itinerary.desc: Drive from Kasol to Amritsar via Pathankot (approx. 5 hours). Check in. Evening visit to the Golden Temple — illuminated at night, reflecting on the Amrit Sarovar. The langar (free kitchen) serves 100,000 meals a day. Wagah Border ceremony if timing allows.

itinerary.title: Amritsar to Delhi — Departure
itinerary.desc: Morning Golden Temple visit at 5 AM for the most peaceful experience. Transfer to Amritsar airport or station → Delhi. Fly home.

included: All transfers by private Innova Crysta
14 nights accommodation (3-star hotels in Rishikesh, Mussoorie, Kasol, Amritsar; guesthouse in Sankri and Taluka; fixed camps on trek)
ITMB-certified English guide for full trek section
Trek equipment: sleeping bag, trekking poles, gaiters

faq.q: How hard is the Har Ki Dun Trek?
faq.a: Moderate-Hard. Daily distances of 7-13 km on forest trails, max altitude 11,680 ft. The terrain is well-maintained and the forest section is beautiful, but Days 6-7 require sustained effort. Suitable for trekkers with some prior experience or good fitness.

faq.q: Why is Har Ki Dun called the Valley of Gods?
faq.a: According to Hindu mythology, the Pandavas (heroes of the Mahabharata) began their final ascent to heaven (Swargarohini peak) from this valley. Locals have worshipped this valley for thousands of years. It's one of the least-visited major treks in Uttarakhand despite its beauty.

faq.q: Is 15 days too long for a first India trip?
faq.a: This is actually an ideal length for experiencing multiple regions. Shorter trips leave you feeling you've rushed. 15 days gives time to slow down, adjust to India's pace, and actually absorb what you see.

testimonials.quote: The Har Ki Dun Valley is the most remote and beautiful place I've been in 20 years of trekking. Zero other tourists for two days. Just us, the guide, and Swargarohini.
testimonials.author: Marcus B.

testimonials.quote: 15 days felt exactly right. We went from jungle trekking to hill stations to Himalayan cafes to the Golden Temple. India in full colour.
testimonials.author: Sophie & Alex R.

testimonials.quote: Booked as a solo traveler via WhatsApp. The team added a second solo traveler to share costs, which worked perfectly. The guide was exceptional throughout all 15 days.
testimonials.author: Ori L.

guide_credentials: ITMB Certified, Har Ki Dun specialist, Wildlife guide certification, 14 years
